doctor_turn: I would just like to start with what brought you in here today to the family clinic?
patient_turn: Yeah, so I've been coughing for a few months now and so that's the problem.

doctor_turn: OK, and how many months have you been coughing for?
patient_turn: Just over two, I would say, and it's been I've been bringing up this yellow, greenish sputum. It's kind of gross.

doctor_turn: OK, and how, how much of the sputum would you say you're bringing up in an average day? Like a teaspoon? Tablespoon?
patient_turn: Oh I see OK. I don't know, maybe a few teaspoons throughout the day.

doctor_turn: OK.
patient_turn: At first I thought I I I had a, a chest infection, but even after antibiotics from my GP, or or my family doctor, It never cleared up.

doctor_turn: OK, and so when did you go on the antibiotics?
patient_turn: Ah so this is about, uh, about a month ago.

doctor_turn: Mhmm.
patient_turn: And yeah, so I went to see my family doctor about the cough then and dad was prescribed some antibiotics. I think azithromycin and it, it still didn't, it didn't do anything.

doctor_turn: OK, I see. And do you find that your cough has been getting worse since then?
patient_turn: Uh, I would say the cough has been about the same.

doctor_turn: But how about the sputum that you're bringing up, has that increased?
patient_turn: Uh, it's about the same, but it definitely I would say there's a pink tinge in the sputum now. Every once in awhile I've noticed more than than before.

doctor_turn: And did this all start after the chest infection or did you have something like this occur before?
patient_turn: Um, yeah, this all started after.

doctor_turn: Was there anything that happened around that time? Any new environment that you were exposed to?
patient_turn: Ah, well, I did go to India a few months ago.

doctor_turn: OK, and how many months was that? Was that also like two months ago?
patient_turn: Yeah, about that, probably closer to three or four, but yeah, right around the time that that I think this, uhm cough started or just before I got back.

doctor_turn: And how often are you coughing in a day?
patient_turn: Ah. a few times an hour.

doctor_turn: Anything that makes your cough go away now you've tried?
patient_turn: Uh, no nothings really helped it go away.

doctor_turn: And is it better or worse? During good morning as opposed to night?
patient_turn: Uh, it's pretty, it's pretty bad all day actually and it's been impacting my sleep because I've been coughing at night too.

doctor_turn: In terms of things that make it worse, anything you do around the house or at work that makes the coughing worse?
patient_turn: Uh yeah, I guess if I'm just moving around a little bit more it might bring it on a little bit more, but it's pretty bad all day.

doctor_turn: Do you have any shortness of breath, runny nose, any chest pain?
patient_turn: Um, no, not really.

doctor_turn: Besides the pain tinged sputum, have you ever coughed up blood, like red blood?
patient_turn: Not anything that was really bright red. More pink.

doctor_turn: Do you remember what kind of place you stayed in India? Was it a, a in a crowded place or were you by yourself?
patient_turn: Well, I mean there is a lot of people around. Yeah, there was not a lot of room where I was was staying, so it was pretty crowded.

doctor_turn: Was there anyone that was ill around you or any exposure to tuberculosis?
patient_turn: Uhm, not that I'm not aware of. I was around my family but, umm, I guess it's possible.

doctor_turn: Did anyone else in your family get sick?
patient_turn: Oh yeah, I spoke to my relative went when I got back and they they didn't tell me about one of my cousins there that was feeling sick.

doctor_turn: Have you been having any fevers or chills?
patient_turn: Yeah, I feel I feel like I've been pretty pretty hot sometimes.

doctor_turn: How about night sweats?
patient_turn: Yeah, the coughs been messing with my sleep, and I've been waking up with night sweats.

doctor_turn: Have you been having any headaches?
patient_turn: Ah no headaches.

doctor_turn: Any unintentional weight loss?
patient_turn: Uh, maybe a few pounds, but um.

doctor_turn: Was that unintentional?
patient_turn: No, I, I was kind of surprised. I thought I would have gained weight 'cause I've been a bit more lazy recently, but the weight seems to be down anyway.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any wheezing sounds when you breathe out, or breathe in?
patient_turn: No wheezing.

doctor_turn: You mentioned no chest pain, right?
patient_turn: Yeah, no chest pain.

doctor_turn: Have any of them been sick?
